question: For a `Book` class with `title` and `author`, overload the `<<` (insertion) operator to allow direct printing of `Book` objects to `std::cout` in a readable format.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1: Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell.
Book 2: Title: To Kill a Mockingbird, Author: Harper Lee.
Book 3: Title: Pride and Prejudice, Author: Jane Austen.
Empty Book: Title: , Author: .

errors: The `operator<<` creates a temporary `std::string` by concatenating title and author before printing. It's more efficient and idiomatic to stream each part directly (e.g., `os << "Title: " << book.getTitle() << ...`).

The `operator<<` uses `std::endl` which flushes the output stream. For general output, using `\n` is more efficient as it avoids unnecessary flushes, which can impact performance, especially when printing many objects.
